title: Israel Completes Southern Lebanon Security Zone Deployment as Ceasefire Violations Mount
summary: Israel announced completion of its 10-kilometer deep security zone deployment in southern Lebanon on April 7, but continued strikes and Hezbollah drone attacks demonstrate persistent military activity in the zone.
contents:
## Southern Lebanon Security Zone Status

### Deployment Complete
Israel announced on April 7 the completion of ground force deployment in southern Lebanon, establishing a 10-kilometer-deep "security zone" along the border. This zone represents a significant territorial control measure extending well into Lebanese territory.

### Ongoing Operations
Despite the ceasefire framework, Israeli military operations continue within and beyond the security zone. Strikes on Habbouch killed 10 people on May 1, and additional strikes across southern Lebanon have claimed more than a dozen lives in the preceding 48 hours.

### Disarmament Claims vs Reality
The Lebanese army claimed in January that it had finished disarming Hezbollah near the Israeli border. However, Hezbollah's media chief confirmed the group successfully introduced forces and weapons into southern Lebanon during the conflict using routes outside Lebanese army control.

### Force Reconstitution
Hezbollah stated it had reconstituted its forces after the previous war with Israel and declared preparedness for a "long battle." The group expressed confidence that the Lebanese army would not confront Hezbollah forces.

### Territorial Assessment
The security zone, combined with Hezbollah's confirmed force reconstitution, creates overlapping military presences in southern Lebanon with significant potential for escalation.
